card_description: Ursula K. Le Guin is a fictional character referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 8 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick bridges his personal experience with the "regression of things" in *Ubik*, proposing that the universe is a "mutually hallucinated" world where humans are "half-alive" (in cold-pac) being roused by the Savior’s voice."
